channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.thesixthaxis.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.thesixthaxis.com

item.title: Morbid Metal early access starts April 8th
item.content:encoded:
Ubisoft and Screen Juice have confirmed that the hack and slash action roguelite Morbid Metal will be launching into Early Access on April 8th. It will have a launch price of $17.99 with a launch discount of 25%. At the the time of launch there will English, German, Spanish, Japanese, Simplified Chinese, and Brazilian Portuguese text and subtitles.

Morbid Metal’s early access will feature three playable characters, 10 enemy types, the first bosses, three biomes, over 60 mid run perks, different character attacks known as protocols, and various effects known as Corpora. There will also be interactables and optional content to discover too. The full roadmap is going to be revealed on April 17th.

Morbid Metal Preview – Forging a slick, stylish, shapeshifting roguelite

In our Morbid Metal preview, Dom said, “With its stylish visuals and slick combat, Morbid Metal could join games like Hades and Risk of Rain as a go-to roguelite. It ties together the instinctive action gameplay of Devil May Cry and Bayonetta with a progression system that rewards commitment and study. I can’t wait to see more when the game arrives in Early Access.”

Source: Steam



https://www.thesixthaxis.com/2026/04/03/morbid-metal-early-access-starts-april-8th/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: State of Decay 3 Alpha playtests have been announced
item.content:encoded:
Can you believe it has been six years since State of Decay 3 was announced for Xbox Series X and PC? At the time, the development team was aiming for a 2021 release which never came with radio silence for a good few years. Then in 2024, State of Decay 3 had a big re-reveal with a new story trailer, with confirmation it would be coming to Xbox Game Pass. And now more news has dropped.

State of Decay 3 Alpha Playtests will be taking place and you can sign up right now through the State of Decay 3 website. In the video, Brett Fitzgerald confirms that from next month there will be alpha playtests taking place. If you are not successful for the first State of Decay 3 alpha playtest, you can hold onto hope that there will be future playtests throughout 2026. The playtests will feature 4 player co-op, new base building and resource strategies, and combat.

In our review for State of Decay 2, Jim wrote, “If you’re looking for a compelling and atmospheric take on the zombie genre that’s more Walking Dead than Left 4 Dead, State of Decay 2 is an imperfect but enjoyable sequel to the superior original. The gameplay loop of scavenging, crafting and killing can begin to wear thin, but the human element is capable of pulling you back in. Tackling the world with others in tow may decimate the game’s carefully constructed atmosphere, but those multiplayer shenanigans will probably be wild enough to distract from the host of technical issues that simply refuse to die.”

Source: YouTube



https://www.thesixthaxis.com/2026/04/03/state-of-decay-3-alpha-playtests-have-been-announced/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
